# CLAUDE.md — Goat Herd Tracker

<!-- SCAN:AUTO:START — Generated by security-scan-prompt. Do not edit this section manually. -->

## Tech Stack

- **Frontend:** Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS (single-file SPA), Google Fonts (Playfair Display, Lato) via @import
- **Backend:** None (client-side only)
- **Data Storage:** localStorage (primary) + Google Sheets sync via Google Apps Script webhook
- **Integrations:** Google Apps Script (data sync + photo upload), Google Sheets, Google Drive
- **Auth:** None
- **Hosting:** Not yet deployed (Vercel recommended per DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md)

## Architecture

### Folder Structure
```
goat-herd-tracker/
  index.html          # Single-file SPA (~5,500+ lines — all HTML, CSS, JS)
  README.md
  DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md
  MAINTENANCE_GUIDE.md
  public/favicon.ico
```

### Key Files
- `index.html` — entire application; HTML structure (lines 1–~1,250), CSS (lines ~1,251–~2,663), JS (lines ~2,664–end)

### Data Flow
localStorage → JS state arrays (goats, treatments, feedRecords, kiddingRecords, breedingRecords) → DOM render via innerHTML; on save, data is written back to localStorage and POSTed to Google Apps Script endpoint (APPS_SCRIPT_URL) for Sheets sync. Photo uploads convert file to base64, compress via canvas, then POST to same endpoint for Google Drive storage.

### External API Calls
- `GET APPS_SCRIPT_URL?action=getAllData` — triggered on manual "Load from Sheets" and on page init
- `POST APPS_SCRIPT_URL` (action: syncFromLocal) — triggered on every data save and on auto-sync interval (every 2 minutes)
- `POST APPS_SCRIPT_URL` (action: uploadPhoto) — triggered when user attaches a photo to a goat record
- `@import https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display...` — CSS font load on page load (no SRI)

## Environment Variables

None defined. The Google Apps Script endpoint URL is hardcoded in `index.html` at line 2786:
```
const APPS_SCRIPT_URL = 'https://script.google.com/macros/s/AKfycby.../exec';
```

## Security Notes

### Active Flags

| # | Severity | Category | Summary |
|---|----------|----------|---------|
| 1 | critical | hardcoded-secrets | Google Apps Script URL hardcoded at line 2786 in public source |
| 2 | critical | no-auth | Apps Script endpoint has zero authentication — any caller can read or overwrite all herd data |
| 3 | critical | xss-innerhtml | 48 innerHTML assignments with unsanitized user-supplied data |
| 4 | moderate | missing-gitignore | No .gitignore file — .env files or secrets could be accidentally committed |
| 5 | moderate | missing-csp | No Content-Security-Policy header or meta tag |
| 6 | moderate | missing-hsts | No Strict-Transport-Security header |
| 7 | maintenance | missing-x-frame | No X-Frame-Options header (clickjacking risk) |
| 8 | maintenance | missing-x-content-type | No X-Content-Type-Options header |
| 9 | maintenance | missing-referrer-policy | No Referrer-Policy header |
| 10 | maintenance | missing-env-example | No .env.example file |
| 11 | maintenance | file-over-500 | index.html is ~5,500+ lines — should be split |
| 12 | moderate | no-rate-limiting | No rate limiting on Apps Script endpoint |
| 13 | moderate | localstorage-sensitive | All farm/herd data stored in plaintext localStorage |
| 14 | moderate | cdn-no-sri | Google Fonts CSS loaded via @import without SRI integrity hash |
| 15 | maintenance | debug-logging | 35 console.log/error statements left in production code |
| 16 | moderate | file-upload-no-validation | Photo upload only checks accept="image/*"; no server-side MIME type or size validation |
| 17 | moderate | no-input-validation | Input validation is client-side only; no server-side enforcement on Apps Script |
| 18 | moderate | cors-open | Google Apps Script endpoints use open CORS by default |
| 19 | moderate | stack-trace-leakage | error.message exposed to user via alert() in sync and photo upload error handlers |

### Accepted Risks

- No rate limiting on Apps Script endpoint — accepted for single-user personal farm app
- localStorage data not encrypted — accepted; single-user, same-origin only

### Resolved

_(none yet)_

## Guardrails

### Universal (apply to all projects)
1. Never hardcode API keys or secrets — use `.env` + `.env.example` from first commit
2. Every API route needs CORS origin validation, input validation, and auth
3. Write RLS/Firestore rules the same day you create a table
4. Never use `innerHTML` with dynamic data — use `textContent` or framework escaping
5. API proxies must restrict destination URLs with an allowlist (SSRF prevention)
6. Auth cookies must contain signed claims (HMAC), not plain booleans
7. If a secret was ever committed to a public repo, rotate it immediately
8. No `console.log` with sensitive data in production

### Project-Specific
9. **Do not add to index.html (~5,500+ lines) — split before adding features**
10. **Use textContent instead of innerHTML — there are 48 existing XSS vectors in index.html**
11. **Add authentication before creating new data-modifying endpoints on Apps Script**
12. **Add rate limiting to any new API endpoints added to the Apps Script**
13. **Add CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy headers before deploying publicly**
14. **Move APPS_SCRIPT_URL out of source code before any public repo push — it is currently hardcoded and visible in git history**
15. **Create .gitignore before adding any .env or secrets files**
16. **Create .env.example before adding new environment variables**

## Guardrails

### Universal

1. **Never commit secrets** — API keys, tokens, passwords, and `.env` files must never be committed.
2. **Never delete or overwrite data** without explicit user confirmation.
3. **Preserve existing behavior** — do not change working functionality unless asked.
4. **Keep changes minimal** — touch only the files and lines necessary for the task.
5. **Do not add dependencies** without explicit approval.
6. **Do not modify build/deploy configs** unless the task requires it.
7. **Always test changes** — verify that modifications work before declaring done.
8. **Respect file structure** — do not reorganize or rename files without approval.

### Project-Specific

> **CRITICAL — Address before any public deployment:**

9. **Move Apps Script URL to environment/config** — The Google Apps Script endpoint URL is hardcoded in the source. Extract it to a configuration variable or `.env`-equivalent so it is not exposed in the public repo.
10. **Sanitize all `innerHTML` usage** — There are 48 instances of `innerHTML` assignment with no input sanitization, creating XSS vectors. Every dynamic value inserted via `innerHTML` must be escaped or replaced with safe DOM methods (`textContent`, `createElement`, etc.).
11. **Add authentication to the Apps Script endpoint** — The Apps Script web app has no auth gate. Add token-based or Google-account-based authentication so the endpoint cannot be called by arbitrary third parties.

> **MODERATE:**

12. **Add a Content Security Policy (CSP)** — No CSP header or meta tag is present. Add one to restrict script/style sources.
13. **Encrypt or scope localStorage data** — localStorage contents are stored in plaintext and accessible to any script on the same origin.

> **ACCEPTED (single-user app):**

14. No rate limiting on the Apps Script endpoint — acceptable for single-user usage.
